questions.question: What are life processes?
questions.answer: The basic functions performed by living organisms to maintain their life on earth are called life processes.

questions.question: Name the basic life processes common to all living organisms.
questions.answer: Nutrition, respiration, transport, and excretion.

questions.question: What is the mode of nutrition in green plants?
questions.answer: Autotrophic nutrition.

questions.question: Define photosynthesis.
questions.answer: The process by which green plants make their own food using carbon dioxide and water in the presence of sunlight and chlorophyll.

questions.question: What is the chemical equation for photosynthesis?
questions.answer: $6CO_2 + 12H_2O \xrightarrow[Sunlight]{Chlorophyll} C_6H_{12}O_6 + 6O_2 + 6H_2O$.

questions.question: Where does the exchange of gases take place in leaves?
questions.answer: Through tiny pores called stomata.

questions.question: What is the function of guard cells?
questions.answer: Guard cells regulate the opening and closing of the stomatal pore.

questions.question: What is heterotrophic nutrition?
questions.answer: A mode of nutrition where organisms depend on other organisms for food.

questions.question: How does Amoeba take in food?
questions.answer: Amoeba takes in food using temporary finger-like extensions of the cell surface called pseudopodia.

questions.question: Name the enzyme present in human saliva.
questions.answer: Salivary amylase.

questions.question: What is the role of Hydrochloric acid in the stomach?
questions.answer: It creates an acidic medium which facilitates the action of the enzyme pepsin.

questions.question: What are villi?
questions.answer: Finger-like projections in the inner lining of the small intestine that increase the surface area for absorption.

questions.question: What is respiration?
questions.answer: The process of releasing energy from food (glucose) is called respiration.

questions.question: Distinguish between aerobic and anaerobic respiration.
questions.answer: Aerobic respiration takes place in the presence of oxygen, whereas anaerobic respiration takes place in the absence of oxygen.

questions.question: What is the first step in the breakdown of glucose?
questions.answer: Glucose (6-carbon molecule) is broken down into pyruvate (3-carbon molecule) in the cytoplasm.

questions.question: Where does aerobic respiration take place in the cell?
questions.answer: Mitochondria.

questions.question: What is ATP?
questions.answer: Adenosine Triphosphate (ATP) is the energy currency for most cellular processes.

questions.question: Name the respiratory pigment in human beings.
questions.answer: Hemoglobin.

questions.question: What are the components of the transport system in human beings?
questions.answer: The heart, blood, and blood vessels.

questions.question: Why is the human heart divided into four chambers?
questions.answer: To prevent the mixing of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood.

questions.question: What is the function of xylem in plants?
questions.answer: Xylem transports water and minerals from the roots to the rest of the plant.

questions.question: What is the function of phloem in plants?
questions.answer: Phloem transports products of photosynthesis (food) from leaves to other parts of the plant.

questions.question: What is transpiration?
questions.answer: The loss of water in the form of vapor from the aerial parts of the plant.

questions.question: What is excretion?
questions.answer: The biological process involved in the removal of harmful metabolic wastes from the body.

questions.question: What is the structural and functional unit of the kidney?
questions.answer: Nephron.
